title: ALT-TAB & Focus
author: pgfiore
body_snippet:
Hi there
I've moved to the latest from a way earlier release. Now I'm facing a small bore and I wonder whether win11 21H2 is involved or not.

Said that I'm a strong supporter of keyboard ALT-TAB to move between windows. Since using 1.77.3, the mRemoteNG window brings to the top, but it does not "get the focus". So, I must click onto it before starting typing.

Please, any suggestions are welcome.
pf

iterations.description: The issue likely stems from incomplete or incorrect handling of window activation messages (e.g., WM_ACTIVATE, WM_ACTIVATEAPP) within the main application window or connection windows when the application loses and regains focus via Alt-Tab, leading to remote session input not being correctly routed or the application itself not being the active window. This directly impacts user experience and productivity.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
Hi

Do you know what version you came from? Because for me this sounds like a manifestation of one of the already known issues like #1408, #2175, #1650, #208 or #1535 which were already introduced a long time ago...

I feel your pain as it annoys me as well, and I hope this gets fixed soon...

comments.author: ryny24
comments.snippet: I thought the beta fixed the ALT-TAB issue but it seems to be back for me. I can't take it anymore :(

comments.author: atlib77
comments.snippet: Would be great if this could be fixed!  I have to use the mouse to give focus to a window within mRemoteNG in order to start typing

comments.author: Eleferen
comments.snippet:
Yes, I acknowledge the issue. This is very annoying :(
I really hope this gets fixed


https://github.com/mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG/assets/15795413/8856e4f6-6c79-4a5a-bbc9-e67c43433b72

What do I mean by this:
1) Set focus on the mRemoteNG
2) Switch to something else. ALT+TAB -> OK
3) We return back to the mRemoteNG. ALT+TAB -> OK
4) Change the tab inside the mRemoteNG
5) Switch to something else. ALT+TAB -> FAIL (Focus remains on the mRemoteNG. To switch to something else, you need to pres...

comments.author: ScaleScale
comments.snippet: is this problem still not resolved?

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The issue likely stems from incomplete or incorrect handling of window activation messages (e.g., WM_ACTIVATE, WM_ACTIVATEAPP) within the main application window or connection windows when the application loses and regains focus via Alt-Tab, leading to remote session input not being correctly routed or the application itself not being the active window. This directly impacts user experience and productivity.
Approach: Investigate FrmMain.cs and ConnectionWindow.cs for WndProc overrides or event handlers (Activated, Deactivated, Enter, Leave) that manage application and control focus. Specifically, look for how WM_ACTIVATE or WM_ACTIVATEAPP messages are handled, or if they are being missed. It might involve ensuring the correct child window or ActiveX control receives focus when the main application window is activated. Potentially, the issue could be related to how the embedded protocol controls regain focus after the parent application is deactivated and reactivated.
